opp.next_step: 15th of june 26 - Confidence : 10% Ask Bihag if he will ask for approval of this project again in September 14th of April 26 - Confidence : 10% BIhag Shah (CPO) called me back : Due to Contract Management project being late SGD to be sold this year or 27 by PAI Partners => No budget for S2P in 26, Bihag mentioned to me that he will do his best to have this project budgeted for 27 so I postponed the close date to March 27 and decrease confidence to 10% as probability to have this budget approved as PAI wishes to sold SGD is low. 9th of April 26 - Confidence : 50% Bihag texted me today that he will call me back (after VM / Text email..) to give me an update. Feb 12, 2026 Call Bihag on 18th of February (he will be back from India) / Validate when he will obtain validation for S2P. 6th of February 2026 - Confidence : 50% Bihag called me for CLM they selected a niche player (100K less expensive over 3 years) / Bihag will communicate me the name when it will be finalized. Bihag should have a Ok for S2P (excluding CLM) before the end of Feb and he is confident that he can take a decision on this before summer. Invitation sent to Bihag for Horizon, inviting also PAI Partners (PE who owns SFD Pharma) 2bd of February 2026 - Confidence : 50% Sent our BAFO on 30th of January before arbitration meeting between Bihag (CPO) and Philippe Pourquèry (CFO) to arbitrate if they can commit on a full S2P instead of only CLM. Our BAFO is valid only for S2P / ARR : 240 K USD / We reduce for CLM part TCO to 260 K Euros vs 200 K Euros for competition (unknown CLM provider, preferred by Legal - Better AI features) our main argumentation is CLM included in S2P (Contract follow up / Risk Analysis / Sourcing connected with contract / Contract connected with eproc / Reporting / Contract efficiency measurement / Supplier evaluation etc..) -> Feedback from Bihag he can accept a premium for Zycus (today 30% was 85% before BAFO) he is aware that we are fighting for him / he will come bacl to us very soon. / Pierre to chase Bihag - Fiona - Valérie if still no feedback by tomorrow 2PM. Jan 27, 2026 Sales Force integration : Ok (Video was sufficient) Last Sales Negotiation call on 29th of January => Need to validate their ability to commit on S2P or only on CLM => Need to adapt number of users 200PU (instead of 50) Call with AL on 30th of January Need to send MSA and OF1 / 2 first week of feb WS to finalize OF2 still need to be scheduled Jan 20, 2026 Following our WS in Paris 1 - Key is Sales Force Integration (Sales Manager attended the WS) => Need to be able to demonstrate it ASAP and Ideally to find a Customer able to make a reference call using SFDC / CLM connector => Need to response on this both topics before 23rd of January 2 - Bihag (CPO) did not attend this meeting he is in India / Fiona (Purchaser and SPOC) Confirmed that a global S2P project has been validated at CFO level but need to receive final validation from PAI partners (PE Owning SGD Pharma) => Pierre / John to call Bihag and have more clarity about this 3 - Several questions following WS to be managed - Mainly around AI (WS well managed by Brice but be carreful if they use our POC intensively they could discover that some of our AI features on IContract are not ready / They are their for Demo purpose but not really working. 4 - Number of users / ball in in SGD court we can not modify definition of our user type => They will come back to us as today they estimate that they need 200 users being able to modify contract (so 200 Power Users / Our proposal is 50) 5 - WS for Implementation to finalize SoW and first legal call still to be shceduled => Pierre to call FIona for Debrief after WS and schedule these calls on 22nd of January. 6 - Due to all these open questions I updated clode date to early march Conclusion : To win 2 major risks / AI not being ready at this stage and SFDC integration - And S2P still not secure . BAFO to be sent by 14th of January (including a solo offer for implementation) / Meeting with Legal WS to be scheduled on 19th of 20th of January (to be confirmed ASAP) Following call with Fiona : Inside information purchasing is still pushing for a global S2P solution we still need to convince legal that we "can make the job" so meeting next week (WS with legal) will be key as well as our BAFO. 6th January 2026 - Confidence : 75% Clarification meeting today - Need to address CLM purely related questions from Valerie and turn her into a Champion. (SGD want reply by 15/12 12pm) We will try to complete but high risk it will be later. Dec 08, 2025 - Confidence : 75% Situation recap Following the meeting with the CPO, you have an informal confirmation that Zycus is selected . Next steps involve legal validation, commercial proposal (prime contractor scenario), and follow-up with stakeholders (Fiona, Bihag, future PM Elise). Action list Legal – Validate ability to cover 100% of needs Action: Call Valérie (Head of Legal). If voicemail, follow up with an email. Objective: Confirm we can cover 100% of her legal/contractual requirements Propose an on-site meeting Reassure her on governance, risk, data, responsibilities, etc. Commercial – Pricing as prime contractor (without Cognizant) Action: Send email with pricing scenario in which Zycus is prime contractor for the services part , without Cognizant. Status: Internal mail sent; Hrishikesh is aligned on timing. Follow-up with Fiona & Bihag Action: Call Fiona and Bihag end of this week for feedback on the new proposal and next steps. Context on Phase 2 and PM Bihag plans to wait for Elise’s return (May/June 2026) before launching Phase 2 / Sourcing of the project. I met Elise (future PM, currently pregnant) at the Christmas market in Normandy and got very positive personal feedback . To call Bihag (CPO) and Fiona purchaser early next week (Tuesday) 11-November 2025 - Confidence : 75% RFP received for CLM (all contracts). answer to be sent by 28th of November - Choice : January / Contract in February / Start of project : March 26. Sent in December a message from Top Management. Ref call with Danone to be scheduled Find a good customer reference on CLM (Danone is apparently ok) 31-October 2025 - Confidence : 75% We were on site Tuesday and Wednesday and RFP is not for S2P but only for CLM as Bihag did not manage the CLM part (it will be also to manage customers contract..Valerie their legal council part of exec board will be the decision maker) we have to be clever in our answer for this first part (so CLM) to be selected and to propose directly a global offer with CLM as a first step to deploy a full S2P suite second phase in 26 with -Sourcing - Third phase in 27 with P2P. Bihag already had validation from his CFO but he needs to convince also PAI partners (Private Equity who owns SGD) as they are under LBO - we have clear follow up and next steps for SGD one of them being to align with Cognizant so if you can set up a call with Sravanth next week would be great. 10-October 2025 - Confidence : 75% Very good call with CPO (Bihag) on 10th of October 2025. Confirmation of WS on 28th and 29th of October. Very positive feedback, non competitive, validated by CFO, Cognizant is supporting us. SFG have on 15th of September a meeting for define next steps, wave of deployment, we should meanwhile receive additional information on additional users for CLM Call to be organized this week to update our sales proposal. 28-August-2025 - Confidence Level:33% Received confirmation from Cognizant Sravanth that Bihag wishes to continue with us asking for a phased approach (starting with CLM) / Sravanth is waiting for a list of questions from SGD then we will have a call in the coming days to address this question and see how we can manage a phased approach knowing that we can take this into account in terms of billing but we want to have a global contract including the whole S2C. 30-July-2025 - Confidence Level:33% Sravanth confirmed that he received all necessary information to finalize his offer he did not received feedback from Bihag at this stage he will chase him and come back to me in the coming days. Viraat called him yesterday still no news from Bihag as he is in vacation in India, so we should have feedback around mid of August not earlier. Today's closing : September / Validation of final budget for a contract closing late October / November 21-July-2025 - Confidence Level:25% Sravanth confirmed that he received ll necessary information to finalize his offer he did not received feedback from Bihag at this stage he will chase him and come back to me in the coming days. Today's closing : September / Validation of final budget for a contract closing late October / November 16-July-2025 - Confidence Level:25% Offer sent to cognizant, to chase Sravanth ensure he has all necessary information to finalize his offer (should be the case as he did not came back but need to have some personal feedback about his perception of our offer and clarify NS to win. Today's closing : September / Validation of final budget for a contract closing late October / November 07-July-2025 - Confidence Level:25% 2nd meeting with detailed presentation on sourcing / contract done - Positive feedback (Cognizant also attended). Need to sync with Cognizant this week to move forward on Service Pricing part. Early phase, project validation in September / October, selection should be done by end of 25. Need to find a way to start only with S2C and P2P in a second phase but to sign a global contract. Bus Case to be sent / follow up call this week to define more precisely next steps and book an on site meeting with Bihag Shah (CPO and Sponsor of this project)
opp.ais_why:
• 14 Avoma meetings on record including on-site Demo Days (28-29 Oct 2025), Technical Session, Final Negotiation, and CLM phase-1 deep dive ‚Äî substantial historical engagement. However the 27 Feb 2026 validation call shows the S2P project has been postponed indefinitely with no new date. Product scope has only Merlin Intake as an AI module; the rest is traditional S2P (iContract, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). The deal is at Vendor Selected stage but currently stalled.
• Vendor Selected stage but now stalled due to indefinite postponement. Cognizant SI partnership active. The 14-meeting engagement was deep but the customer focus was traditional S2P, CLM, and IT roadmap rather than AI-first. The single AI SKU (Merlin Intake) in scope is the only AI signal across an otherwise traditional deal shape.
• High confidence on the engagement/stall picture: the rich 14-meeting trail plus explicit project-postponement evidence is unambiguous. Only one AI product in scope means the deal is traditional-procurement-led even when active. Score 4 (boundary DOWN per rubric) ‚Äî would move only if the project restarts with AI scope expansion or remain at 4 reflecting the historical engagement depth despite no AI lead.

items.quote: Bihag will communicate me the name when it will be finalized

items.area: PE owner (PAI Partners) decision timeline on selling SGD and approving 2027 S2P budget
items.quote: PAI Partners seems to wish to sell SGD / in this case some projects could be frozen
final commercial arbitrage pivoted on price premium, no shared savings model

sources: Avoma 'SGD - Zycus Intro' 20 Jun 2025, Bihag Shah
metrics.narrative: Discovery call 20 Jun 2025: Bihag Shah described manual, error-prone procurement across European plants (RFQs, order acknowledgments, POs via email and Excel, manual SAP entry) and contract management with no central repository, driving the S2P case. Workshop and demo engagement through Oct 2025 reinforced qualitative pain (oversight risk, no spend visibility, no contract lifecycle control) but the buyer never committed a quantified baseline (spend under management, headcount, cycle time). The final commercial arbitrage (29 Jan 2026) pivoted entirely on price premium versus the CLM niche player, not on a shared ROI model, so a quantified value case remains the gap. Quantifying savings for the September budget conversation is the move.

sources: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
champion.narrative: Bihag Shah (Group CPO) is a strong, sustained champion across 12 months (Jun 2025 to Feb 2026): direct CFO access, positioned Zycus as the only S2P finalist, secured informal CFO approval in January 2026, and negotiated aggressively to keep Zycus in the game ('he is aware that we are fighting for him', Next_Step__c 2 Feb 2026). His advocacy is not cooling by choice, the stall is external (PE-owner budget freeze). The current risk is capacity, not loyalty: on 27 May 2026 he asked to wait for the year's budget decision and has been quiet since, and if PAI Partners delays or declines 2027 budget he cannot advance S2P regardless of advocacy. Fiona Zhou (Lead Buyer, SPOC) is a consistent secondary supporter for a global S2P solution.

sources: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
competition.narrative: For the standalone CLM scope Zycus lost 6 Feb 2026 to an unnamed niche CLM player that was roughly 100k cheaper over three years (competitor TCO ~180 to 200k versus Zycus ~360k for 200 power users), covered ~98 percent of requirements versus Zycus ~90 percent, and was rated higher on AI by Legal (source: Avoma 29 Jan 2026, Next_Step__c 6 Feb 2026). Zycus's wedge is single-vendor S2P integration (CLM connected to sourcing, eProcurement, and supplier risk) which Bihag championed but could not defend against the price gap on CLM alone. For the remaining S2P modules there is a second, unnamed finalist ('the second supplier whom we have finally listed there', 29 Jan 2026) and Pierre observed IT leaning to SAP/Ariba, though the buyer did not confirm that. The named Competitors__c rivals (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) never surfaced in any call and are dormant. The single live current threat is the CLM niche player, but the deal's real blocker is budget, not a competitor.

sources: Avoma 'SGD - Zycus Intro' 20 Jun 2025, Bihag Shah
identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed operational pain: fragmented, manual procurement and contract management across SGD's plants with no central repository, manual expiry tracking, and oversight risk in thin-staffed European sites (discovery 20 Jun 2025, Bihag). A second, sharper pain surfaced 19 Jan 2026: after SGD's sales team adopted an SAP AI contract-generation module, the business now needs contracts to originate in Salesforce and flow seamlessly to the CLM, or adoption will fail. Inferred pain from the deal shape: SGD is under an LBO and the PE owner is optimizing for a sale, so any large multi-year IT commitment is politically hard right now, which is the true reason the project froze rather than any functional gap. Pain owner is Bihag (procurement) with Legal (Valerie) owning the contract-lifecycle pain.

sources: OpportunityContactRole Valerie Breon-Normand, Group General Counsel
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics were in motion before the freeze: MSA and Order Forms 1 and 2 were to be sent early February, an SoW workshop and first legal call were being scheduled, and Cognizant was the SI/prime-contractor partner for implementation (with a Zycus solo offer using a subcontracted French-speaking project manager as the alternative). Valerie Breon-Normand (Group General Counsel) leads legal validation and is on the exec board, making Legal a required sign-off. No contracting has advanced since the Feb 2026 postponement, so paper process is dormant. What would restore visibility is a confirmed 2027 budget from the relevant stakeholder that reopens the MSA/SoW track; contract mechanics themselves are not the blocker, budget authority is.

sources: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
economic_buyer.narrative: Philippe Pourquery (Group CFO, who also heads IT) is the internal economic buyer: he approved CLM budget in 2025, held in-principle agreement on S2P in January 2026, and was set to arbitrate the S2P commitment with Bihag on 30 Jan 2026 at 11:30 (source: Next_Step__c 2 Feb 2026, Avoma 29 Jan 2026). The decisive authority, however, sits ABOVE the CFO: private-equity owner PAI Partners must approve S2P because SGD is under an LBO and PAI is weighing a sale of the company. Bihag on 29 Jan 2026: 'we have all agreements internally is done, but we do not have official go for all our IT projects yet, it is still pending at our shareholders level.' The EB is mapped and positive, but the real budget gate is the PE owner, whom Zycus has never reached.

sources: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah
decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence: discovery Jun 2025, source-to-contract presentations Jul 2025, on-site two-day Demo Days 28 to 29 Oct 2025, a CLM RFP round Nov 2025, BAFO and legal deep-dive Jan 2026, then a final commercial negotiation 29 Jan 2026. Bihag described the process on 29 Jan: parallel workstreams (IT integration, commercial negotiation, legal) converging to an internal February decision meeting with Bihag, Valerie (Legal), and IT to select on price, functionality, and IT fit. CLM and S2P were decoupled: CLM had pre-approved 2025 budget and moved independently, while the remaining three S2P modules needed fresh management and shareholder approval. That approval chain (CFO in-principle, then PAI Partners shareholder go) is the gating process, and it stalled at the PE-owner level in Feb-Apr 2026.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation ran through a Paris workshop and structured RFP/BAFO rounds; the buyer's criteria are explicit. Single-platform integration is the Zycus wedge (CLM connected to sourcing, eProcurement, supplier risk versus best-of-breed), which Bihag argued internally to justify a premium. Salesforce integration became a top criterion after SGD's sales team bought an SAP AI contract-generation module (Deep Diving workshop 19 Jan 2026, Cyril and the SGD lead: contracts must flow smoothly between Salesforce and the CLM). SAP on-prem / S4HANA connectivity, SSO, user provisioning, and DR/RTO-RPO were validated in the 28 Jan technical session. AI capability and price were the two criteria on which Zycus lost the standalone CLM piece: Legal rated the niche competitor higher on AI at a lower cost.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. Strong on paper for the stage, but it's stalling — the 'what could lose it' block is the real story.

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Bihag Shah (Group CPO) is a strong, engaged champion with sustained 12-month advocacy, direct CFO access, and proven ability to drive internal consensus and keep Zycus the only S2P finalist. He is not cooling by choice, the stall is external (PE-owner budget freeze). Current risk is capacity: since his 27 May 2026 defer he has gone quiet, and if PAI Partners declines 2027 budget he cannot advance S2P regardless of advocacy. What is still missing is a second executive thread that survives independently of him.

The decisive authority, however, sits ABOVE the CFO: private-equity owner PAI Partners must approve S2P because SGD is under an LBO and PAI is weighing a sale of the company. Bihag on 29 Jan 2026: 'we have all agreements internally is done, but we do not have official go for all our IT projects yet, it is still pending at our shareholders level.' The EB is mapped and positive, but the real budget gate is the PE owner, whom Zycus has never reached.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Bihag deferred: 'I would like to wait for the decision in this year budget'; Zycus lost the CLM scope on price and AI, and no buyer-initiated engagement since 27 Feb 2026.
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — Bihag deferred: 'I would like to wait for the decision in this year budget'; Zycus lost the CLM scope on price and AI, and no buyer-initiated engagement since…

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 4.0 / Moderate: the buyer engaged deeply but led with traditional procurement and contract needs, and preferred a competitor's AI on CLM. Calls confirm AI curiosity (Merlin agents, regulation-drafting agent discussed in the 19 Jan workshop) without AI being the decision driver.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS_Score 4.0 / Moderate: the buyer engaged deeply but led with traditional procurement and contract needs, and preferred a competitor's AI on CLM.

bullets.full: Edge over the competition — Zycus was shortlisted Jan 2026 to two finalists but lost the standalone CLM scope 6 Feb 2026 to an unnamed niche CLM player (~100k cheaper over 3 years, ~98 percent fit vs ~90 percent, higher-rated AI per Legal). The remaining S2P scope kept Zycus as a finalist against one other unnamed S2P supplier, with IT (per Pierre) leaning to SAP/Ariba though the buyer did not confirm it. The named Competitors__c rivals (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) never appeared in any call and are dormant. Recency-weighted, the CLM niche player is the strongest current threat (won CLM Feb 2026), but the deal's decisive blocker is not a competitor, it is the PE owner's frozen budget. No new call evidence this run, so threat levels are carried forward.

Decisive authority sits with the PE owner above the CFO; Zycus has no thread to that level
Lost standalone CLM on a ~100k / 3-year price gap and a 30 percent-plus premium; S2P commercials must be re-justified

blocks.read: Light — they haven't committed much yet.
blocks.title: Customer commitment

items: Send Bihag a French S2P reference proof point to hold the relationship warm over summer. (by 10 Jul)
Build the single-platform integration-tax and Merlin AI business case for Bihag's September budget push. (by 17 Jul)
Keep the CFO (Philippe Pourquery) and Fiona threads alive with a light value update so a restart does not depend only on Bihag. (by 24 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: SGD Pharma — Vendor Selected · $150K · closes 17 Mar

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 34 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 73 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 55 vs stage-expected 60.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 20 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 25 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 52 is anchored on the stage win (73), nudged by commitment 25 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 34. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 20 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 4.0 / Moderate: the buyer engaged deeply but led with traditional procurement and contract needs, and preferred a competitor's AI on CLM. Calls confirm AI curiosity (Merlin agents, regulation-drafting agent discussed in the 19 Jan workshop) without AI being the decision driver.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain well articulated (manual procurement, oversight risk, no contract repository) and a workshop estimate of ~40 percent time saved, but no confirmed baseline or ROI; the final decision pivoted on price, not a shared value model.

items.change: Owner emailed Bihag before summer break proposing a September or October restart; awaiting reply
Bihag replied he prefers to wait for this year's budget decision and keep in touch (buyer defer)
Close date pushed 2026-06-24 to 2027-03-17 and forecast moved Best Case to Pipeline as budget was frozen and PE sale flagged
S2P project postponed indefinitely with no new date on the validation call
Amount reduced 190k to 150k; CLM scope lost to an unnamed niche player on price and AI

deal_movement.summary: The deal peaked in Jan 2026 (BAFO, technical validation, final negotiation) then froze in Feb 2026 and has drifted since. Recent activity is entirely rep-side outreach against a budget-frozen, PE-gated hold; the buyer's last substantive input was the 27 Feb postponement, and Bihag's only touch since is a 27 May defer to a 2026 budget decision.

items.detail: No S2P budget in 2026; project frozen pending PE owner approval for 2027, probability low
PE owner PAI Partners weighing a sale of SGD; large IT commitments may be frozen until the ownership question resolves
Decisive authority sits with the PE owner above the CFO; Zycus has no thread to that level
Lost standalone CLM on a ~100k / 3-year price gap and a 30 percent-plus premium; S2P commercials must be re-justified
Close date is a placeholder (Mar 2027) with no committed decision date; deal dormant through summer

critical_signals.text: SGD has no S2P budget for 2026 and the project is frozen until the private-equity owner decides whether to fund it for 2027, a low-probability outcome.
On the 27 February validation call Bihag postponed the S2P project indefinitely, showing an IT roadmap that pushed it out with no new date.
Zycus lost the standalone contract-management scope to an unnamed niche player that was about 100k cheaper over three years and rated higher on AI by Legal.
The real decision authority is owner PAI Partners, above the CFO, and Zycus has no relationship at that level.
After SGD's sales team adopted an SAP AI contract module, seamless Salesforce-to-CLM contract flow became a make-or-break requirement.

champion_strength.source: Avoma 29 Jan 2026; Next_Step__c 2 Feb 2026, 27 Feb 2026, 14 Apr 2026; Task 2026-05-27
champion_strength.summary: Bihag Shah (Group CPO) is a strong, engaged champion with sustained 12-month advocacy, direct CFO access, and proven ability to drive internal consensus and keep Zycus the only S2P finalist. He is not cooling by choice, the stall is external (PE-owner budget freeze). Current risk is capacity: since his 27 May 2026 defer he has gone quiet, and if PAI Partners declines 2027 budget he cannot advance S2P regardless of advocacy. What is still missing is a second executive thread that survives independently of him.

items.expected_effect: Keeps Zycus top-of-mind for the September budget review without pressuring a frozen buyer.

items.expected_effect: Arms the champion with a quantified case to defend S2P funding and rebuild the AI credibility lost on CLM.

confidence_signals.summary: Buyer confidence has decayed: from an active BAFO and negotiation in Jan 2026 to an indefinite postponement (27 Feb), a budget freeze (14 Apr), and a polite defer to keep in touch (27 May). Momentum is dormant, gated on an external ownership decision.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 23 Jun 2025, 375 days old; in Vendor Selected 12+ months. Close date 2027-03-17 is 257 days out (a placeholder set 14 Apr 2026 when budget was frozen, not a slip). Last substantive two-way buyer call 27 Feb 2026 (126 days ago), when Bihag paused recording to show the IT roadmap and postponed S2P with no new date. Since then only rep-side touches: Bihag's 27 May 2026 reply 'I would like to wait for the decision in this year budget' and the owner's 1 Jul 2026 pre-summer-break email proposing a Sept/Oct restart. Cold for 60+ days with the buyer explicitly deferred forces Off Track regardless of Vendor Selected stage.
evidence: Buyer reply 27 May 2026: Bihag to Pierre, 'Thanks for the invitation, I would like to wait for the decision in this year budget. Let us keep in touch' (source: Task 2026-05-27 Clari email received)
Rep outreach 1 Jul 2026: Pierre emailed Bihag before summer break checking whether discussions could restart in September or October (source: Task 2026-07-01 Clari email sent)

Buyer call 27 Feb 2026: Bihag shared the IT roadmap and postponed the S2P project indefinitely with no new date; recording paused before the screen share (source: Avoma 'Validation of your S2P project' 27 Feb 2026)

Budget freeze confirmed 14 Apr 2026: no S2P budget in 2026 because the contract-management project is late and PE owner PAI Partners wishes to sell SGD in 2026 or 2027; Bihag will try to budget S2P for 2027, probability low (source: Next_Step__c 14 Apr 2026)
Field history: on 14 Apr 2026 CloseDate moved 2026-06-24 to 2027-03-17 and ForecastCategoryName moved Best Case to Pipeline (source: OpportunityFieldHistory)

Commercial negotiation 29 Jan 2026: Bihag, 'with yesterday's price I can tell you it is not gonna be you if the price maintains like that for standalone' (source: Avoma 'Final Sales negotiation SGD' 29 Jan 2026, Bihag Shah)

Pipeline forecast is indefensible: Vendor Selected 12+ months with budget frozen and a placeholder close date awaiting an external PE ownership decision; the honest category is Omitted until 2027 budget is secured
north_star_verdict.headline: Vendor Selected since Feb 2026 but S2P budget frozen; owner PAI Partners weighing a sale of SGD blocks approval. No substantive buyer engagement since the 27 Feb 2026 postponement; Bihag deferred re-engagement to a 2026 budget decision and the deal is dormant through summer.

flags: Real blocker is PAI Partners' sale decision above the CFO; align the PE owner on S2P transformation value rather than only chasing Bihag, who cannot move budget alone.
Zycus lost CLM on price and AI to a niche player; for any S2P restart lead with the Merlin agentic AI story Legal (Valerie) wanted and quantify the multi-vendor integration tax.
Engagement is single-threaded on a frozen champion; keep the CFO (Philippe Pourquery) and Fiona threads warm independently so a September restart does not depend solely on Bihag.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is externally blocked at the private-equity ownership level, not on functional fit; win-strategy must reach the owner, rebuild the AI narrative, and diversify beyond a single frozen champion.

customer_preference.evidence: Bihag deferred: 'I would like to wait for the decision in this year budget'; Zycus lost the CLM scope on price and AI, and no buyer-initiated engagement since 27 Feb 2026.

competitive_position.summary: Zycus was shortlisted Jan 2026 to two finalists but lost the standalone CLM scope 6 Feb 2026 to an unnamed niche CLM player (~100k cheaper over 3 years, ~98 percent fit vs ~90 percent, higher-rated AI per Legal). The remaining S2P scope kept Zycus as a finalist against one other unnamed S2P supplier, with IT (per Pierre) leaning to SAP/Ariba though the buyer did not confirm it. The named Competitors__c rivals (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) never appeared in any call and are dormant. Recency-weighted, the CLM niche player is the strongest current threat (won CLM Feb 2026), but the deal's decisive blocker is not a competitor, it is the PE owner's frozen budget. No new call evidence this run, so threat levels are carried forward.

Zycus was out-positioned on AI in the CLM evaluation, where Legal rated the niche competitor higher, so the agentic story is under-landed for this buyer.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is strong on integration and delivery but exposed on price and AI, the two axes on which Zycus lost the CLM scope. The remaining S2P case rests on single-platform value and the CFO's prior in-principle agreement, both currently frozen by the PE-owner budget decision.

hard.ais_why:
• 14 Avoma meetings on record including on-site Demo Days (28-29 Oct 2025), Technical Session, Final Negotiation, and CLM phase-1 deep dive ‚Äî substantial historical engagement. However the 27 Feb 2026 validation call shows the S2P project has been postponed indefinitely with no new date. Product scope has only Merlin Intake as an AI module; the rest is traditional S2P (iContract, iRisk, iSource, iSupplier). The deal is at Vendor Selected stage but currently stalled.
• Vendor Selected stage but now stalled due to indefinite postponement. Cognizant SI partnership active. The 14-meeting engagement was deep but the customer focus was traditional S2P, CLM, and IT roadmap rather than AI-first. The single AI SKU (Merlin Intake) in scope is the only AI signal across an otherwise traditional deal shape.
• High confidence on the engagement/stall picture: the rich 14-meeting trail plus explicit project-postponement evidence is unambiguous. Only one AI product in scope means the deal is traditional-procurement-led even when active. Score 4 (boundary DOWN per rubric) ‚Äî would move only if the project restarts with AI scope expansion or remain at 4 reflecting the historical engagement depth despite no AI lead.
